topic: Batch post-integration review of WIN-058/058b/059/060/061/062 after EXP-181 (registry_integrity_notes acknowledge-without-fix paragraphs) integrated in commit 2fa4b57 and subsequent c3 patches in 4426819
our_argument_summary: EXP-181 appended three registry-integrity-notes paragraphs to WIN-058, WIN-058b, and WIN-062 detail_evidence, crediting the dome author's 2026-04-17 machine-readable disambiguation (claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes in commit 6e06efb) while arguing the visible prose and headline count remain unchanged. The three blocks are mutually consistent in their core framing (three acknowledge-without-fix instances in commit 6e06efb: methodology.json timestamp_scope, methodology.json registry_counts, claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes). The batch's verdicts are unchanged (058 Misleading, 058b Not Demonstrated, 059 Std Model Explains, 060 Std Model Explains, 061 Self-Contradicted, 062 Misleading). WIN-059/060/061 were not touched by EXP-181 and their cycle-2 reviews (2026-04-14) remain valid — wins.json git log confirms no subsequent modifications to those three entries. The critique itself is defensible and the credit-where-due framing is appropriate. However, four issues introduced or amplified by the EXP-181 integration merit attention: (a) a broken cross-reference link from WIN-058 to WIN-058b (onclick="return false" without the accompanying getElementById call kills default anchor navigation), (b) an unsupported "status downgraded from confirmed" claim for the bedrock-kappa entry, (c) an undercounted "three concessions" framing that silently excludes the ai_manifest.json registry_semantics self-caveat shipped in the same 24-hour window, and (d) an overstated "aggregate count still reflects it" claim for the WIN-058 collision (only one of the two WIN-058 blocks is badged CONFIRMED; the other is PENDING INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION, so the collision does not double-count against the 69-confirmed tally the way the WIN-062 collision does).

relevant_files: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-181.json — three-patch expansion that landed the paragraphs
monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/REACTIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-17.json — identified the gap EXP-181 was written to close

docs/index.html — checked anchor targets: id="win058" exists (1), id="win058b" exists (1), id="detail-058b" does NOT exist, id="timestamp-error" exists (1), showTab("timestamp") is the correct tab call
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No dome-side monitor.py domain for WIN-058 theta-rule or WIN-058b bedrock-kappa or WIN-062 Tesla-wave-speed. The EXP-181 integration is purely prose/evidence augmentation; no code_analysis tag changes, no aggregate count changes, no predictions.json or sections.json reaches. Integrations commits 2fa4b57 (EXP-181) and 4426819 (WIN-058 c3 patches) are sequential — the c3 review of WIN-058 post-dates EXP-181 integration and already reflected the appended prose in its detail_evidence_length 3346 -> 7978 fingerprint delta.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — this batch review inspects prose/link integrity, not monitor.py code.
data_sources_used: live wins.html (fetched 2026-04-18 per EXP-181) — badge states verified: WIN-058 theta-rule CONFIRMED, WIN-058 bedrock-kappa PENDING, WIN-062 (x2) both CONFIRMED
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The "acknowledge-without-fix" framing is deliberately neutral on intent (EXP-181 anticipated_objections #3 grants this explicitly). The "three in one commit" framing is a narrow scope (commit 6e06efb only) that excludes ai_manifest.json registry_semantics shipped in adjacent commits 5021eec/a57e731 — EXP-181 holes_deferred acknowledges this deferral.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Integration simplified EXP-181 patch P1's onclick handler: original text was onclick="document.getElementById('detail-058b')?.setAttribute('open','');return false" (href="#detail-058b") and integrated text is onclick="return false" (href="#win058b"). The href fix was necessary because id="detail-058b" does not exist in docs/index.html, but the simplification dropped the auto-expand behavior while KEEPING return false — which cancels the default anchor jump. Result: clicking "WIN-058b" in WIN-058's prose does nothing visible. This is a regression introduced by the integrator, not by the expansion author.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: This batch review is a meta-review of an expansion integration; the underlying WINs inherit their tags unchanged. For reference: WIN-058 coordinates identity (none, or hardcoded per c3 recommendation), WIN-058b bedrock-kappa (none, no monitoring), WIN-059 NMP deceleration (none), WIN-060 SAA drift (none), WIN-061 Schumann (none), WIN-062 Tesla wave (none). No API/fetch for any.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Five of six batch members are tagged relabels_standard=true (058, 059, 060, 061, 062). Only WIN-058b has relabels_standard=false, because a universal magnetic-gravity coupling constant has no standard-physics analog (though the correlated SG/magnetometer signal IS standard — electromagnetic artifact contamination per Crossley et al. 2013, flagged in WIN-058b c2).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: All six batch members carry post_hoc=true. EXP-181 does not change this; the registry_integrity_notes acknowledgement is itself documentary concession to the post-hoc nature of the catalog as a whole.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: All six carry derives_from_dome=false. θ = −lonE is a coordinate convention, κ = 1.677 is a measurement ratio, NMP/SAA drift is extrapolated from external geomagnetic datasets, Schumann suppression follows ionospheric physics, Tesla 1.574c is a waveguide phase velocity. No dome-parameter derivation path for any of the six.

holes_found.description: Broken cross-reference link in WIN-058 detail_evidence. The integrated EXP-181 P1 paragraph contains WIN-058b. The onclick="return false" cancels the default anchor jump, and there is no JS body to replace it, so clicking the link is a no-op in the browser. The original EXP-181 patch used onclick="document.getElementById('detail-058b')?.setAttribute('open','');return false" (self-consistent: auto-expand target, then suppress default navigation because the element IS already scrolled into view by the setAttribute call). The integrator fixed the href target (detail-058b does not exist in docs/index.html; win058b does) but dropped the getElementById call without also removing "return false". This is a live regression: a reader who trusts the cross-reference sees nothing happen when they click. Verified against rendered docs/index.html (id="win058b" present, but onclick="return false" kills the jump).
holes_found.recommendation: Either remove onclick entirely so the default href="#win058b" anchor jump works (simplest), or restore the getElementById-expand pattern pointing at the correct element id for WIN-058b's <details> block if that element has a usable id. Simpler fix recommended because (a) it requires no JS dependency and (b) matches how WIN-058b's counter-link (href="#win058" with no onclick) works. One-line patch.

holes_found.description: "Status downgraded" claim is unsupported by change-file evidence. WIN-058's integrated block asserts the bedrock-kappa entry's status was "downgraded in the same commit to pending_independent_verification, with an explicit honest_caveat". WIN-058b asserts "the author downgraded the κ claim from the headline 'confirmed' tally to pending inside the same commit". The change file chg-20260417-1620-004.json documents only the ADDITION of registry_integrity_notes; it does not record a prior "confirmed" state for win-058-bedrock-kappa, and the visible WIN-058 bedrock-kappa badge on live wins.html reads PENDING INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION (as EXP-181 itself documents). Without evidence that claim_index.json previously carried status="confirmed" for this key, "downgraded" is speculative. A defender can legitimately challenge: "There was no prior confirmed state to downgrade from — the entry was newly created as pending in 6e06efb."
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "status downgraded in the same commit to pending_independent_verification" with "the entry's status field was set to pending_independent_verification when the key was added, with an explicit honest_caveat". For WIN-058b, replace "the author downgraded the κ claim from the headline 'confirmed' tally to pending inside the same commit" with "the new claim_key registers this entry as pending_independent_verification from the outset, with an honest caveat that independent reproduction is required". Preserves the genuine credit (the author added the honest caveat) without claiming a state transition we cannot evidence.

holes_found.description: "Three concessions in one commit / 24-hour window" framing silently excludes ai_manifest.json registry_semantics. EXP-181 frames exactly three acknowledge-without-fix instances: methodology.json timestamp_scope, methodology.json registry_counts, claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes. But change file chg-20260417-1620-005.json (commits 6e06efb,5021eec,a57e731, same 24-hour window) documents ai_manifest.json adding a registry_semantics block that explicitly labels each count as "display-level bookkeeping" and warns "do not treat the display ratio as a substitute for claim-by-claim prospective audit." That is a fourth parallel self-caveat at the metadata layer. EXP-181 holes_deferred acknowledges this gap ("tightening of ISS-1087's eventual patch wording") but the integrated WIN-058/058b/062 prose still asserts "three" without hedge. A rigorous dome defender can reply: "Your own review files flag a fourth registry_semantics self-caveat in ai_manifest.json from the same commit window. Why is the count three?" The count is the central rhetorical beat of the pattern paragraph — undercounting is a structural rather than cosmetic issue.
holes_found.recommendation: Change "three instances" / "three parallel documentary concessions" to "at least three" in all three WIN blocks, and either (a) add a parenthetical noting ai_manifest.json registry_semantics as a parallel instance with a link to ISS-1087 / upcoming patch, or (b) keep the hedge and leave the registry_semantics citation for the later ISS-1087 resolution. Option (a) is the cleaner fix because the three blocks are the only place this pattern is cataloged in WIN prose; ISS-1087 resolution may not propagate here.

holes_found.description: "Aggregate count still reflects it" is overstated for WIN-058 (contrast: fair for WIN-062). The WIN-058 block says "the site's headline ('69 confirmed') is tallied against the visible-prose registry, not the disambiguated API. The acknowledgement therefore stops at the machine layer; the user-facing surface still carries the duplicate label and the aggregate count still reflects it." But EXP-181 verification itself documents the two WIN-058 blocks as having DIFFERENT badges: "The 1.67 Bedrock Induction Ratio" badge PENDING INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION, and "θ = −lon_E: Unified Angular Coordinate Rule" badge CONFIRMED 2026-03-28. A headline "69 CONFIRMED" tally reasonably counts only CONFIRMED-badged entries — so only the theta-rule entry contributes, and the bedrock-kappa entry does not inflate the count. The WIN-058 collision is therefore a labeling collision, NOT a tally-inflation problem. Contrast with WIN-062: both blocks are CONFIRMED, so that collision DOES inflate the tally (by +1). The WIN-058 block's "aggregate count still reflects it" language borrows the WIN-062 rhetorical force without the badge evidence to support it.
holes_found.recommendation: In WIN-058's block, distinguish the two failure modes: (a) collision: two different claims under one visible label (this is the integrity problem) — NOT dependent on both being confirmed; (b) tally inflation: only applies to WIN-062, where both blocks are CONFIRMED. Rewrite the relevant sentence as: "The user-facing surface still carries the duplicate label for two different claims — one confirmed, one pending. The disambiguation is machine-only." Drop the "aggregate count still reflects it" phrase from the WIN-058 block (it's fine to keep in WIN-062's block where both blocks are confirmed).

holes_found.description: Commit attribution is inconsistent across the three blocks. WIN-058 says "three instances in one commit (6e06efb, 2026-04-17)". WIN-058b says "Three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window". WIN-062 says "three parallel acknowledge-without-fix move shipped inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17". These are not contradictory (the three named instances are all in 6e06efb, so the narrower claim subsumes the looser one), but they give a reader comparing the blocks the impression of imprecise provenance. A defender can ask: "Is it one commit or a 24-hour window? Your own WIN blocks don't agree."
holes_found.recommendation: Standardize to "three instances in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)" across all three WIN blocks. The narrower framing is both more accurate and more forensically useful.

holes_found.description: WIN-058 onclick="showTab('timestamp');return false" for the Timestamp Error anchor drops the default anchor jump to id="timestamp-error" but relies on the tab switch alone to make the content visible. If the Timestamp Error tab has content above the id="timestamp-error" landing block, the reader arrives at the top of the tab rather than at the specific April-2026 update block the link targets. Not a broken link (showTab succeeds and id="timestamp-error" is a valid DOM anchor), just a worse-than-possible landing. Same pattern in all three blocks.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider refactoring to onclick="showTab('timestamp')" (no return false) so the default anchor jump to id="timestamp-error" runs after the tab switch. Lowest priority — cosmetic UX. Verify the jump actually lands correctly in the switched-tab DOM before batching.

stronger_arguments.description: The acknowledge-without-fix pattern becomes more forceful if we can demonstrate at least one prior acknowledge-without-fix instance BEFORE the 2026-04-17 wave. If the pattern is not isolated to one 24-hour burst but extends back into the V50 era, it moves from "three concessions one day" to "standing methodology." The Timestamp Error tab's pre-2026-04 material may already contain candidate instances (e.g., the original 94.5% accuracy display vs individual-WIN-badge accuracy audits).
sources: Timestamp Error tab April 2026 update block (should catalog chronologically)

stronger_arguments.description: For WIN-062 specifically, the duplicate-Tesla-block tally inflation is a concrete, verifiable +1 to the "69 confirmed" number. If we can establish the "69 confirmed" figure WITHOUT the WIN-062 duplicate is 68, that's a small but testable claim of display-count inflation. Useful for the Part 2b aggregate-count analysis.
sources: live wins.html line 734 + line 816 (both WIN-062 CONFIRMED 2026-04-04)
docs/api/current/claim_index.json win-062-tesla-wave-speed (single entry after normalization) — implicit count of 68 if deduped

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's ai_manifest.json recommended_read_order is itself a double-edged sword for the "acknowledge-without-fix" claim. If an AI auditor follows the read-order and lands on claim_index.json before wins.html, they see the disambiguated registry and the collision is resolved for them. BUT — the read-order is ONLY for AI auditors. Humans don't receive the manifest. The structural asymmetry (machine-layer correctness, visible-prose decay) is therefore itself an argument that the dome author is optimizing for AI-auditor approval over human-reader correctness. This framing is stronger than the current "acknowledgement stops at the machine layer" because it names the audience segmentation explicitly.
sources: EXP-181 P1 paragraph quoting the read-order

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author did identify the WIN-058 label collision and the WIN-062 pseudo-duplicate and shipped machine-readable disambiguation/normalization inside 24 hours of the reactive-change wave. That is real work, and EXP-181's "Credit where due" framing is appropriate. The claim_key slugs (win-058-theta-rule, win-058-bedrock-kappa, win-062-tesla-wave-speed) are well-formed, the honest_caveat on win-058-bedrock-kappa is genuine documentary progress, and the ai_manifest.json read-order gives AI auditors a path around the visible collision. A fair reviewer must credit these moves. EXP-181 does.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The credit does not save the underlying WINs's verdicts because (a) the three machine-layer fixes live only where AI auditors are told to look and the site's headline count ("69 confirmed") still renders against the visible-prose registry, (b) the bedrock-kappa entry is still badged pending on the visible page and the theta-rule entry is still visibly labeled WIN-058, (c) the Tesla WIN-062 duplicate still contributes two CONFIRMED badges to the visible tally, and (d) none of this addresses the core post-hoc / derives_from_dome critiques our review relies on for the 058/058b/062 verdicts themselves. The verdicts remain unchanged.

cross_win_consistency.verdict_consistency: All six verdicts unchanged (058 Misleading, 058b Not Demonstrated, 059 Std Model Explains, 060 Std Model Explains, 061 Self-Contradicted, 062 Misleading). No verdict drift from EXP-181 integration.
cross_win_consistency.tag_consistency: All six retain post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false. Five retain relabels_standard=true; WIN-058b retains relabels_standard=false. Monitoring="none" for all six. No tag drift from EXP-181.
cross_win_consistency.pattern_paragraph_wording_drift: Wording drifts across the three EXP-181 blocks: WIN-058 "three instances in one commit (6e06efb, 2026-04-17)", WIN-058b "Three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window", WIN-062 "three parallel acknowledge-without-fix move shipped inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17". Not contradictory, but inconsistent precision. Flagged as minor hole above.
cross_win_consistency.cross_reference_link_health: WIN-058 → WIN-058b link BROKEN (onclick="return false" kills anchor jump). WIN-058b → WIN-058 link WORKING (no onclick). WIN-058/058b/062 → Timestamp Error tab link partially working (tab switch succeeds, but anchor jump to id="timestamp-error" is cancelled by return false — minor UX issue).
cross_win_consistency.untouched_WINs: WIN-059, WIN-060, WIN-061 were not touched by EXP-181 (verified via git log on data/wins.json since 2026-04-14). Their cycle-2 reviews remain valid and no post-integration re-review is required for those three.

citations_verified: id="win058" present in docs/index.html (target for WIN-058b → WIN-058 cross-link)
id="win058b" present in docs/index.html (target href for WIN-058 → WIN-058b cross-link; link still broken by onclick)
id="timestamp-error" present in docs/index.html (target for all three blocks' Timestamp Error anchors)
showTab("timestamp") is the correct tab call per docs/index.html showTab("...") inventory
EXP-181 verification_report claim_key entries (win-058-theta-rule, win-058-bedrock-kappa, win-062-tesla-wave-speed) match the verbatim quotes in the three WIN blocks
commit 6e06efb confirmed as the commit that introduced methodology.json (chg-001) and claim_index.json registry_integrity_notes (chg-004)
citations_failed: "Status downgraded" claim (WIN-058 and WIN-058b blocks): no change-file evidence for a prior confirmed state of win-058-bedrock-kappa. Flagged as moderate hole #2.
"Three concessions" count (all three blocks): excludes ai_manifest.json registry_semantics (chg-005), which is a fourth parallel self-caveat in the same 24-hour window. Flagged as moderate hole #3.
citations_unchecked: Whether the live 2026-04-18 wins.html "69 confirmed" count is still accurate today (2026-04-18 later in the day — no re-fetch performed in this review)
Whether id="timestamp-error" is at the top of the timestamp tab's visible area (would determine whether the onclick="showTab('timestamp');return false" lands in a usable place)

deeper_analysis: EXP-181 was a response to REACTIVE-AUDIT-2026-04-17.json's identification of chg-20260417-1620-004 as the one uncovered gap in the five-change reactive wave. The expansion's scientific content (crediting the dome author's documentary acknowledgement, preserving the visible-prose critique, framing the pattern) is well-constructed and holds under adversarial pressure. The primary weaknesses introduced by the integration are operational rather than scientific: a broken onclick link, an unsupported "downgraded" inflection, an under-counted pattern tally, and one overstated tally-inflation claim (WIN-058) that belongs only with WIN-062. None of these flip any verdict or undermine the acknowledge-without-fix thesis. The recommended_action is minor_edit because all four moderate-or-major items are one-line textual or punctuation fixes. The adversarial-advocate reading — that an AI auditor following ai_manifest.json sees a resolved registry, that the machine-layer fix IS the fix — is granted explicitly by EXP-181's anticipated_objections and rebutted in the patches themselves. That rhetorical structure holds. WIN-059/060/061 inheritance check: no post-integration issues; their cycle-2 reviews remain valid. Overall, the EXP-181 integration succeeded at its primary goal (closing ISS-1117's gap about missing registry_integrity_notes engagement) and the remaining issues are tractable micro-patches rather than structural rewrites.
